name: Velvet Fudge Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. Extend the height of a 1-quart souffle dish 3 inches above a dish with a band of double thickness aluminum foil. Secure the ends of the foil by folding them together, taping, or fastening with paper clips.
2. In a 2-quart saucepan, mix together 1/2 cup sugar, gelatin, salt, and red wine.
3. Beat egg yolks slightly; stir in chocolate chips. Add yolk mixture to gelatin. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture boils.
4. Chill the pan in a bowl of ice and water or in the refrigerator, stirring occasionally, until it mounds slightly when dropped from a spoon (about 20-30 minutes). The mixture should be slightly thicker than beaten egg whites. If it becomes too thick, place the pan in a bowl of hot water, stirring constantly, until the proper consistency is reached.
5. In a large mixer bowl, beat egg whites to become foamy. Gradually beat in sugar until stiff and glossy (do not underbeat).
6. Gently fold the thickened gelatin mixture into the meringue.
7. Beat 1 1/2 cups whipping cream in a chilled small mixer bowl until stiff. Fold this into the meringue mixture.
8. Carefully turn the soufflé into the prepared dish. Refrigerate until set, about 8 hours.
9. Just before serving, run a knife around the inside of the foil band and remove it. Garnish with sweetened whipped cream.
